title: lowercase (deprecated)
text: lowercase (deprecated) Convert a string to lowercase. Note: The lowercase statement is deprecated. It has been superseded by $lowercase function, which is applicable to all component types. lowercase Source , Target Example: lowercase vString1, vString2 Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Source String Content to convert to lowercase. Target String Destination of converted content. Return Values None Use Allowed in all component types. Description The lowercase statement converts the contents of Source to lowercase, then places the result in Target . Conversion is on a character-by-character basis as defined by Unicode. Note: Locale-based processing rules are not applied when using lowercase . If this is desired, use $lowercase . The following example converts the contents of $1 to lowercase: vString1 = "ABC" lowercase vString1, vString2 ; result: vString2 = "abc" Copy Related Links Case Conversion You can use ProcScript statements and functions to convert string from uppercase to lowercase or from lowercase to uppercase. The default conversion behavior is determined by the values of NLS assignment settings and ProcScript functions, which control locale-based processing. $lowercase Convert a string to lowercase. $uppercase Convert the specified string uppercase. uppercase (deprecated) Convert the specified string to uppercase.
